title: PtyExitEvent
description: pty.exit — server-pushed event emitted once when the shell process behind pty_id terminates. exit_code is the wait-status exit code (or -1 if the process died from an uncaught signal); signal is populated when the shell died from a signal rather than exit(N), e.g. SIGKILL from the connection-close cleanup path. Daemon flushes pending pty.output before emitting pty.exit so the FE can replay the final bytes.

exit_code.description: Process exit code, or -1 if the process was killed by a signal.

signal.description: Signal name (e.g. "SIGTERM", "SIGKILL") when the process died from a signal.
